data.description:
## Reproduction Steps
- tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py:207 (the ONLY fork-convergence test) asserts status_a[seed] in ("in_progress","blocked") and status_a==status_b. It never asserts WHICH UUID wins, so a replay resolving the fork by wall-clock/insertion-order (instead of the documented deterministic-by-UUID rule) still passes — the doctrine the test exists to prove is not exercised.
- L198 asserts only any(...-CREATE.json) survives the union, not that BOTH ta and tb CREATE files survive.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Compute the two STATUS event UUIDs; derive the expected winner by the documented UUID rule; assert status_a[seed] equals that SPECIFIC value
- [ ] Assert BOTH ta and tb CREATE event files survive the union
- [ ] Test fails if the tie-break rule is changed to non-UUID
data.title: Fork/union tie-break test must assert the UUID winner, not set-membership
